new.title: Take Action with ChatGPT: Update CRM Records & Access more Objects and Engagements
new.description: HubSpot connector for ChatGPT now writes to CRM from chat, letting you create/update records and log activities plus access orders, invoices, line items, segments and engagement history right in ChatGPT. Bulk creates up to 10 and audit/security notes included for safer, faster workflows.

new.title: HubSpot Developer MCP server (local) for App and CMS Development Now in GA
new.description: HubSpot unveils GA of the Developer MCP Server, linking local agentic tools to HubSpot for code, CMS assets, and serverless tasks. Developers can build and iterate apps, manage CMS assets, run serverless functions, and debug with natural language prompts.

new.title: Update Your CRM Directly from Claude and ChatGPT
new.description: HubSpot's AI connectors got a major upgrade in February. Both the Claude and ChatGPT connectors can now create and update CRM records, log activities, and access engagement history — all without leaving your AI chat window. You can create contacts and deals, log notes and tasks, update deal stages, and tap into your full engagement history (emails, calls, meetings, and notes) to get context-aware insights and take action on them instantly. ChatGPT users also gained access to products, line items
new.availability: All HubSpot customers across all tiers with a paid Anthropic subscription (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise). ChatGPT connector available to all HubSpot customers with an OpenAI account.

new.title: Breeze Assistant Prompts Reimagined
new.description: The Breeze Assistant prompt experience got a full refresh. You can now use @Mentions to pull in CRM records directly within your prompt, save and reuse prompts with placeholders and tags, and browse categorized suggestions (Summarize, Create, How do I, Prepare) to discover what Breeze can do. Saved prompts can be organized by team function — Customer Success, Marketing, Sales, Support — and assigned to specific assistants or made available across all of them. The "Prepare" category is particular

new.title: Personalization App Now Supports Website Pages
new.description: The Personalization app now covers website pages in addition to landing pages and CTAs. Using Variants, you can create audience-specific versions of your website pages in one place without duplicating pages or manually editing individual modules. You can set up variants manually for full control, or use AI-assisted setup for a faster start. Performance is tracked across all variants in the Analyze tab, so you can see what's resonating with each audience without hunting across different tools.
new.availability: Content Hub Professional and Enterprise, Marketing Hub Professional and Enterprise.

new.title: Granular Create Permissions for CRM Objects
new.description: Admins can now control whether users can create new CRM records — separately from their ability to edit them. Previously, any user with edit access to Contacts, Companies, Deals, Tickets, or Custom Objects could also create new records. That forced a tradeoff between enabling your team to do their jobs and keeping your database clean. With this update, you can grant or restrict create access independently for each object type. Head to Users & Teams to update permissions. Note that this permissio

new.title: Branded Email Templates
new.description: You can now apply your Brand Kit directly to HubSpot's native email templates. When creating a new email, simply select your brand from the dropdown in the template library to have your logo and colors applied automatically. If you need to update your Brand Kit, you can do that from the same dropdown without leaving the email creation flow. This requires a Brand Kit to be set up in your portal first. Once it is, getting on-brand emails out the door takes minutes instead of hours. Learn more abou

new.title: Build Custom Reports on Form Submissions
new.description: The Custom Report Builder now supports form submission data as a source. You can combine Forms, Form Submissions, and Form Analytics data with other objects like Contacts and Companies to build detailed reports on form performance. Useful reports you can build include submissions over time, submissions by source or campaign, conversion rates, new vs. returning contact breakdowns, and submissions filtered by lifecycle stage or ICP tier. Reports can be added to any dashboard. Note: There may be a
new.availability: Content Pro and Enterprise, Marketing Pro and Enterprise, Ops Pro and Enterprise, Sales Pro and Enterprise, Service Pro and Enterprise.

new.title: Additional Company News Signals in Buyer Intent
new.description: Four new signals are now available within Buyer Intent under Company News: Industry Recognition, Office Closure, Physical Expansion, and Regulatory Approval. These signals surface directly on company timelines so your team gets relevant account news where they already work. Use these as triggers in workflows and lead scoring to prioritize accounts with momentum — or as context for timely, relevant outreach. All four signals are toggled on by default, so you'll start seeing them on company record

new.title: Conditional Scoring with "AND" for Health Scores
new.description: Health scores now support conditional scoring rules using "and" logic, meaning points are only awarded when multiple criteria are met simultaneously. There are two types: cross-property rules (Fit scoring), which combine multiple property criteria for a single object, and event-specific rules (Engagement scoring), which award points when a specific event happens under specific conditions (for example, a call that lasted at least 15 minutes). Previously, you had to work around this limitation usi
new.availability: Professional and Enterprise Customer Platform, Service Hub Professional and Enterprise.

new.title: HubSpot Link Previews in Slack
new.description: When you paste a HubSpot record link into Slack, you'll now see a structured preview of that record's key details — no extra clicks required. Clicking the preview opens a side panel in Slack with the full record and related threads. Supported objects include Deals, Tickets, Companies, Contacts, Emails, Blog Posts, Reports, and Dashboards. Each preview surfaces the most relevant fields for that object type (for example, a Deal shows name, amount, stage, priority, and owner).

new.title: Upgraded Social Analytics
new.description: The Social Analyze tab has been redesigned with a new set of curated, ready-to-use reports organized into four categories: Grow your brand, Drive engagement, Track leads from social, and Optimize your content. You can filter by account, network, and time range, and the underlying data has been upgraded to ensure consistency with custom reports you build yourself. Note: Historical data in the new Analyze tab is only available from July 1, 2025 onward (or from the date your social account was conn
new.availability: Professional and Enterprise Customer Platform, Marketing Hub Professional and Enterprise, Marketing+ Pro and Enterprise.

new.title: Campaign Automation in Workflows
new.description: Campaign properties and events are now available as triggers and criteria in workflows. You can automate based on campaign-level data like budget, spend, revenue, and owner — and trigger actions when a contact is influenced by a specific campaign. This opens up use cases like alerting your team when a campaign overspends, automatically following up with influenced contacts via tailored sequences, or routing tasks based on campaign owner. Check out our knowledge base article on how to use campaig

new.title: Sales Meeting Notetaker Updates
new.description: The Sales Meeting Notetaker received several reliability and usability improvements based on beta feedback. The notetaker now joins at the meeting start time (rather than 2 minutes early) and will wait up to 5 minutes for the host to arrive. It also now correctly joins rescheduled meetings, and transcripts include speaker names for easier post-meeting review. Admins can enable the notetaker via Settings > Tools > Calling > Meetings > Meeting Notetaker. It works with Zoom, Google Meet, and Micros

new.title: Participant State Management for Marketing Events
new.description: You can now update a contact's registration and participation status for marketing events directly from their contact record — no imports required. From the Marketing Events CRM card, simply click Edit next to the registration or participation status and select the updated value. Note: This card is only visible for contacts already registered to a marketing event. You cannot use it to associate a new contact with an event. Participation status cannot be updated once it is set to "Attended," and

new.title: 2,000+ Apps and 2.5M+ Active Installs: New and Recently Updated Apps
new.description: Explore an expanding ecosystem with over 2,000 apps and 2.5 million active installations. Browse newly added and recently updated integrations to extend HubSpot's capabilities. *available to all hubs and tiers

new.title: Latest updates to Breeze Assistant
Latest updates to Data Enrichment and Buyer Intent
new.description: HubSpot unveils richer data enrichment and buyer intent with expanded EU contact coverage, smarter enrichment from sparse data, and a 10% lift in intent signals. New transparency controls plus higher match rates ensure scalable, trustworthy enrichment across Smart CRM.

new.title: The HubSpot Technology Partner Program: Clear Tiers & Real Recognition
Latest updates to Customer Agent
new.description: Breeze Customer Agent unveils safer onboarding, flexible rollout controls, and stronger testing to boost confidence before going live. Highlights include four weeks free access, channel aware settings, email citations, automated detection, and workflow driven deployment for smarter, hands-off support.

new.title: Latest updates to Prospecting Agent
Code Sharing for UI Extensions
new.description: You can now share code between UI extensions. Previously, if your App Cards, App Homes, and Settings pages required the same code types, utilities, and components, you had to copy the code for each instance. This meant that whenever you made updates, you had to revise every copy of that code separately. In the unified developer platform, your app cards, app homes, and setting pages are all part of the same app. With npm workspace support, your UI extensions can share the same codebase. Define yo

new.title: Associate Payments with Tickets
new.description: Payments can now be directly associated with tickets. In the UI, you can add a CRM card for Payments to the Tickets record page. Then, you can use this CRM card to directly connect the ticket you’re on to a Payment. If you’re using the Tickets API endpoint for associations, you can use the HubSpot-defined associationTypeId of 1355. To associate a payment with a ticket, use the associationTypeId of 1354.

new.title: OAuth Install Event Logs
new.description: We’re adding OAuth install event logs to the Developer Platform, providing enhanced install visibility for all apps built on version 2025.2 or newer. What’s new or changing? This functionality is available for apps built on the Developer Platform v2025.2+. Please migrate your app if you wish to leverage install event logs. To access logs, go to Development > Monitoring > Logs in your HubSpot account. Set up observability integrations if you’d like logs exported automatically. This change helps d

new.title: Tech Partner Program Tiering
new.description: Technology Partner tiers help customers identify app developers who consistently deliver high-quality experiences in the HubSpot ecosystem. Tiers: Partner, Rising, Leading, and Premier are assigned at the developer level (not per app) and appear on Marketplace listings and filters. Tier upgrades are evaluated quarterly based on performance over the previous 12 months, with downgrades beginning semiannually in 2027. Developers can track their current tier, progress toward the next level, customer

new.title: Connected Apps: New Connections Home & Connection Insights
new.description: Connections Home is a new centralized page for managing your integrations. It provides a comprehensive view of apps connected to your account, apps requiring immediate attention, and apps installed by other users across your organization. This update also includes a new Connection Insights section that provides a detailed overview of app activities, Academy content, new API call usage, and new Record Insights that track how your apps interact with HubSpot and CRM data. To view the new Connection

new.title: Custom Events Are Now Available to the Pro Platform
new.description: Custom events are now available to all Pro customers. Previously an Enterprise-only feature, Custom events let you track any behavioral data that matters to your business, such as product usage, purchasing behavior, offline interactions, third-party platform activity, and changes to properties inside HubSpot. Note: Pro customers are limited to 10 million event occurrences per month. Enterprise customers are limited to 30 million event occurrences per month. Both Pro and Enterprise customers can

new.title: Public Beta: Conversation & Workflows Ownership Updates
new.description: We’re introducing additional functionality to conversation-based workflows in Service Hub Professional and Enterprise subscription tiers in HubSpot Workflows & Inbox. These betas enhance ownership flexibility by adding a new “No Owner” option to the Assign Conversation Owner action and extending the Rotate record-to-owner action to work with conversations. Together, these updates give support teams more structured, automated control over conversation routing and handoffs, while respecting inbox

new.title: A tiered framework built on impact
new.description: We're introducing four partnership tiers: Partner, Rising, Leading, and Premier. Each has transparent requirements and benefits that grow with your business. Your tier is determined by three factors: These metrics are designed to provide partners with transparency on how to deepen their partnership with HubSpot.

new.title: Benefits that match your impact
new.description: As you graduate into higher tiers, you unlock: Every tier builds on the one before it. The more customer value you create and the more revenue you influence, the more HubSpot invests back into your growth.

new.title: Marketplace visibility for top partners
new.description: Rising, Leading, and Premier partner tiers are now displayed in the HubSpot Marketplace. Customers can filter by tier to find integrations that meet higher standards of quality, support, and strategic alignment with HubSpot. The HubSpot Marketplace receives 1 million customer visits per month. Now your tier helps you stand out.

new.title: A grace period to adapt
new.description: We're giving everyone time to adjust to the new program. For the 2026 program launch, there will be no tier-downs. The first tier-down review happens in Q1 2027. You'll have until the end of the year to understand the new metrics, invest in the right areas, and grow into the tier that reflects your ambition. The marketplace review and premier certification tier requirements will also go into effect in Q1 2027.

new.title: If you're already an app partner
new.description: You're automatically part of the Technology Partner Program. Here's what happens next: Important: Tier evaluations happen quarterly (on the 15th of the first month each quarter). You'll receive direct communication from HubSpot with your tier status and next steps.

new.title: If you're a HubSpot customer
new.description: The Technology Partner Program makes it easier to: Look for tier badges when evaluating integrations. They're a signal of partnership depth and ongoing investment.

new.title: If you're building a new app
new.description: The Technology Partner Program gives you a clear on-ramp and growth path from day one. You'll know exactly what to focus on at each stage to unlock upleveled benefits as you grow. Want hands-on support? Consider attending our next Developer Platform Playground webinar on March 19.

new.title: Catalyst: a new fast track for high-potential partners
new.description: We're also introducing Catalyst, a 6-month accelerator for a select group of high-potential technology partners. Catalyst supports two types of partners: Catalyst partners receive a named partner manager, technical consulting with solutions architects, and focused support to demonstrate customer impact and move up tiers faster. Eligible partners will be invited directly; no application required. Learn more about Catalyst → Technology Partner Program overview – See the full guide on tier requirem

new.title: AI-Powered Prospect Research
Agent Customization & Flexibility
Selling Profiles
Email Capabilities
Personalized Email Drafting & Follow-Up
Seamless Integration
Workflow & CRM Enrollment
UI Extensions: p_* wildcard enables cards for all custom objects
Breeze Studio agents upgraded to GPT-5
Sync Single Sign On (SSO) Settings to Sandbox and Developer Accounts
Enhanced Quote Design with HTML Editing
Custom events can now be deleted
New Sandbox Listing Page
Pre-rendering warnings now appear in Design Manager
Breeze Customer Agent Now Supports Omnichannel Deployment via Custom Channels API
New Campaign Asset Type Associations via Campaigns Public API
Company Lifecycle Stage Properties
Partner Search Analytics
Marketplace Updates
Custom event updates
Public Beta: Forecast Read API
Public Beta: Securely generate a preference page URL
new.description:
UI Extensions
UI Extensions now supports a p_* wildcard in card configuration, letting you target all custom object types in a portal without listing individual schema names (like p_my_object) or portal-specific fully qualified names (like p123456_my_object). This removes a major blocker for public apps, since custom object names vary by customer portal—apps can now work across any customer’s custom objects without hardcoding object IDs. To use it, set the object target in your card config to p_

new.title: New Resource: Legacy CRM Card Converter
new.description: To support developers migrating from Classic CRM Cards to the new App Cards, we’ve released the Legacy CRM Card Converter, a lightweight tool and example designed to simplify the transition process. The resource provides: The card converter is intended to accelerate planning and development as you prepare for the October 31st, 2026, sunset of Classic CRM Cards. Explore the tool here: https://github.com/HubSpot/ui-extensions-examples/tree/main/legacy-card-converter

new.title: Developer Tasks and App Migration Paths
new.description: Now the Developer Platform surfaces actionable insights directly on the Developer Overview for all HubSpot Accounts. Developers can view task cards highlighting whether any of their apps are eligible for migration to the new Developer Platform, with clear guidance on which apps can and cannot be migrated. Previously, the Developer Overview lacked actionable next steps, leaving users uncertain how to maintain or upgrade their apps. These new insights enable clear, actionable guidance, reducing mi

new.title: HubSpot IP Ranges API
new.description:
You can now dynamically access HubSpot's public IP ranges for your projects and firewalls. With this API, you can automatically update your firewall rules and enhance your security monitoring. You can retrieve the set of IP ranges using this endpoint:
https://api.hubapi.com/meta/network-origins/2025-09/ip-ranges
This API does not require authorization. The response will include a list of IP ranges and the HubSpot services using those IPs. See the documentation for full details.

new.title: New Filter by Object Type ID for v3 Lists API
new.description: The /crm/v3/lists/search endpoint in the v3 Lists API now supports filtering lists by object type using the objectTypeId parameter in the POST request body. See additional details on what specifically changed, below: This new feature enhances list segmentation by allowing filtering of lists by HubSpot CRM object type. Applicable to CRM segments (part of Lists), especially for Marketing Hub and Main Hub products. Additionally, this change enables tighter integration for custom reporting and workf

new.title: Template Selection for CPQ Quotes via API
new.description: You can now specify a custom template when creating CPQ quotes through the Quotes API, rather than relying on the default content template. This unlocks full control over quote design and structure for any workflow that programmatically generates quotes. During creation, the API will associate the quote to the selected template and pull in any additional template data, while still prioritizing values explicitly set on the quote. Note that templates can only be chosen at creation time and cannot

new.title: Standard Sandbox Updates: New Limits, New Capabilities, and Legacy Sunset
new.description: HubSpot is rolling out a more powerful Standard Sandbox experience, alongside clearer data limits and a planned sunset of Legacy Standard Sandboxes.

new.title: Public API for Uninstalling an app released to GA
new.description: The previously announced API for uninstalling an app from a HubSpot Account has been released from beta into general availability. This endpoint allows app builders to remove their app from a HubSpot Account, which removes any app features set up for the app, including removing any configured app cards, as well as unsubscribing any configured webhooks for that account. See the documentation for more details.

new.title: Updates to associations to the Cart object
new.description: You can now create associations between carts and custom objects, and configure custom association labels and limits for the cart object. This improvement allows you to tailor the cart object to your specific business needs and workflows. This update allows integrations to update associations using the Associations API, as well as allowing super admin users to update associations by editing the data model in their account. Additionally, users can associate carts to deals directly from record pag

new.title: Breeze Assistant can now query your Custom event and App event data
new.description: Breeze Assistant can now query the custom website events and app events you've configured in your HubSpot Account. You can now get answers about these events directly from Breeze Assistant, helping you:

statusChanged.title: Introducing HubSpot CLI v8.0.0
statusChanged.description: HubSpot CLI v8.0.0 introduces breaking changes with a Node 20 minimum, removal of deprecated commands and legacy behaviors, and dropped support for older project versions. A major cleanup aligning with platform updates, effective February 9 2026.

updated.title: Legacy Developer Accounts Migrating to the Unified Developer Platform
updated.description: Hubspot unveils a migration of legacy developer accounts to a unified Developer Platform, consolidating tools, apps and integrations into one Hubspot account. The upgrade brings newer features, requires post-upgrade access review, and offers an opt-in path or automatic conversion during a phased rollout from March 9 to 31, 2026.
